description: Competition in popular Olympic activities is incorporated in this exciting game using decimals from 10ths to 1000ths. Over 40 problems using addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division of decimals are included. Olympic activities including the javelin, discus, shot-put, and pole vault allow students to earn points toward winning a gold, silver, or bronze medal. For 2-4 players plus a referee.

Over 400 problems for each of the four basic math operations are included in this exciting fractions game. Competition in ten popular Olympics events are used to motivate players. For example, the javelin, discus, shot-put, pole vault, and many more activities allow students to earn points towards winning a gold, silver, or bronze medal. For 2-4 players and a referee.

Two players or teams can score touchdowns, field goals, and experience other activities found in football games as they practice multiplication skills. For example: 6X2 = 12 which represents a fumble with no points scored.

Players will begin to understand the problem solving details that go into having a party as they play this game. Included are activities that use addition and subtraction of time, money, and liquid measurement. For example: 4 cups of punch equal one quart. How many cups of punch equal one gallon? The time is 1:00p.m. and the party starts in 15 minutes. What time does the party start? 2-4 players Grades 2-6

Students become masterminds as they use inductive and deductive reasoning with this exciting game. Using colorful bears, students build color pattern riddles that their opponent must solve on the transparent gameboard. This activity is great for whole class competition or for use in learning centers. 2- players or whole class participation. Grades 1-6

Here's an action packed board game designed to meet a variety of educational needs while tapping into America's favorite pastime... Baseball! As players build their own teams, they use their own baseball cards or the baseball cards included in the game to answer problem solving questions. Included are integrated reading, math, and geography skills covering time zones, states and capitals, major league cities, rivers, lakes, and oceans. Lots of baseball trivia is included. There are five levels of difficulty, allowing players of different ability levels to play together. 2-4 players or teams. Grades 2-12

Play golf while you learn to compute combinations of addition, subtraction, multiplication, division, cubes, square roots, and more. Using 60 cards in the deck along with golf terminology, players draw "stroke" cards to create equations which solve a target number. After nine rounds of play the game is over and the player with the lowest score wins the game.

boardgame: Renegade Legion: Leviathan – The Wake of the Kraken

Renegade Legion: Leviathan Scenario Book - 64 pages with 15 new scenarios.

This book includes a background story of the INS Kraken and it's new commander, Rear Admiral Luther Catalus.  Catalus has run afoul of Imperial politics and has been sent on a suicide mission.  The book covers the campaign fought by the INS Kraken and it's escort squadron in 15 interlinked scenarios.  The campaign is designed to be played through with the same units and track damage between scenarios.  Rules are included to cover resupply and repair.  Complete profiles of the key members of the Kraken Squadron are also included.

The book also provides the designs of several ships, many of which are also included the Renegade Legion: Leviathan Capital Ship Briefing.  Other ships are new to this book.  These ships are:

description: The object of the game is to advance all of your stakes to the finish line before your opponent in order to win. The game can be played opposite each other or side-by-side. When playing opposite each other, the game cards must be placed in the card bay so that the green block is at the bottom and at the player’s right. Playing side-by-side, both cards must be placed with the green block at the bottom. Stakes must be advanced vertically only. There are three (3) versions to this game: RANDOM, MIX, and FIX. Random is the basic version and should be learned first....

Here's a great, competitive, fun-filled game. Each player chooses a colored pawn and follows the track around the board by spinning and moving to a specific number in either the ones, tens, hundreds and thousands place. The winner is decided by the first player who makes it once around the board and follows their colored alley to the center of the board. Grades 1-4

Here's a great, competitive, fun-filled game. Each player chooses a colored pawn and follows the track around the board by spinning and moving to a specific number in the thousand, millions, up to the hundred millions place. The winner is decided by the first player who makes it once around the board and follows their colored alley to the center of the board.

Here's a great, competitive, fun-filled game. Each player chooses a colored pawn and follows the track around the board by spinning and moving to a specific number in either the tenths, hundredths, and thousandths place. The winner is decided by the first player who makes it once around the board and follows their colored alley to the center of the board. Grades 4-7

This uses a similar idea to the popular 'parking' puzzles but in a strategy game. Each player has 5 vehicles - 3 normal cars (2x1 spaces), a bus (3x1 spaces) and a Smart car (1x1). On your turn you have 3 choices: 1) Place a vehicle face down, 2) Turn one of your vehicles face up and choose its direction of travel without altering its position (so the larger vehicles can choose two directions, the Smart can choose four), 3) Move one of your vehicles one space in its direction of travel. 
The aim is to be the last player to be able to move. Even if one cannot move at some stage of the game if your way is subsequently cleared you can still move then.

This is a collection of 12 games playable with the same components released to commemorate the 150th anniversary of Gauss' death. The games are mainly 2 player. They use a set of wooden blocks which come in lengths of 1 to 6. A die is used in many of them to determine the length of the piece to be placed. There are variants on connecting-sides-of-the-board, placement-within-a-limited-space, moving- across-the-board, blocking and capturing games. 8 are adult games, 4 children's games. There are also an additional 2 adult games by Peter Lewe.

Over 400 real-life challenges are included in this great game. Players learn to purchase furniture, clothing, appliances and more. Extensive drill in computing percentages, fractional discounts, keeping financial records and reconciling accounts is covered. Great for home use, and learning centers. 2-4 Players Grades 5-12

In the center of the board, there are wobble puddings in a clearing. Players roll a die and move their 'little monster' to collect them and bring them back to their home. But the puddings are guarded by a dragon, which may be moved when the players roll its symbol. Players can be send home like in pachisi, with the capturing player also capturing the pudding.

The game ends when all 10 puddings are distributed. They have 1-3 points on the bottom side, and the player with the highest total wins.

A description from Greg Costikyan:

Aki Jarvinen has posted cards and rules for GameGame 1.0… which he pitches as an attempt to do for games what Understanding Comics does for comics: that is, to provide insight into the nature of games in a game, in the same what that Understanding is both “about comics” and a comic.

Thus, his cards are things like “Goal” and “Environment” and “Victory/End Condition.” There’s both a coherent set of rules for determining victory and a roleplaying element: players must improvise what the game they are creating is like, e.g., describing what their “Victory/End Condition” is–but they can only have one if they have the relevant card.

Version 2.0 recently became available at the website below.

Website:  http://gamegame.blogs.com/

In Henne Berta (Hen Berta in English) the player feeds a hen with grain and in return gathers eggs to a little basket. The first player that gets 3 eggs to basket is the winner. The game contains a wooden board, large wooden pieces and little baskets for every player.
RPM is a race simulation released by Grow during the 80's in Brazil.

How long did John Glenn orbit the earth? This and many more curious questions are included in this fun-filled game. History, math, problem solving, and trivia are incorporated into three levels of difficulty so that students at different ability levels can be challenged as they play together. 2-4 Players Grade 3-8

Place Search is a great activity in a four-sided domino format. Players match words with their corresponding numerals. Two levels of difficulty cover numbers through the thousands place. 1-4 players. Grades 3-12

Place Search is a great activity in a four-sided domino format. Players match words with their corresponding numerals. Two levels of difficulty cover numbers through the hundred millions place. 1-4 players. Grades 3-12
Using the tic-tac-toe format and three levels of difficulty, students are immersed in competitive drill with equivalent fractions.
This game contains 45 dominoes and five game ideas using halves, thirds, fourths, fifths, sixths, and eighths. It's great for home use or learning centers.

Most likely the first "real" wargame (by today's standards) released in Brazil, it had lukewarm reception during the 80's and has been discontinued. 
The game simulates a battle between two fictitious armies, using an hexagonal board and two sets of rules: basic and advanced.
This is the same Avalon Hill's Tactics II (1958), with a redesigned hexgrid mapboard.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
"Jon Compton's tactical hex and counter game of WWII combat. This set focuses on Germany versus Poland at the start of the war."

Each hex on the map represents an area approximately 100 yards across. Each playing piece represents about 40-60 men (a platoon) or half a dozen vehicles or towed weapons . . . For ease of play, the game scales are approximated. Time is also abstracted, thus turns represent several minutes.

The FOXHOLE system is simple to learn and fast playing that focuses on the invasion of Poland. Its a good diversion from the more heavy fair of historical board-wargaming.

FOXHOLE SET 2 includes:
-Ten new scenarios, six exclusively with this set, and four incorporating elements from Set 1.
-Twenty-four unit cards
-Over one hundred playing pieces -Tactical Battles Map A - and One rule book

This game is played on a hex board, you have 3 Figures, the first (King) can move only 1 step (if you lose that piece you are out of the game), the second piece can move 2 step and the third can move 3 step. When you move on a hex a stone fall, so the hex is no more usable. When you must move one figure on an empty hex, the first time you can turn over the figure, the second time the figure goes out of the game. If is the King all your figures go out. The last player on the board is the winner.

Incorporate baseball with addition, subtraction, multiplication and division problems. With three levels of difficulty, students from first to sixth grade can have fun in pairs or small groups as the play baseball. Get the whole class involved by creating an overhead transparency from the black line master that is included. This game may also be adapted for weekly homework assignments and remedial math programs. 2-4 players or 2 teams.

It's the first game of a trilogy of two-player Napoleonic campaign games.
Napoleon's Italian campaigns take place during the time of Napoleon's campaigns from 1796 through 1800 in Italy.
The game contains the maps, 252 counters, charts and tables
Players journey around the Roman Empire, conquering garrison forts in a race to proclaimed "Caesar"! The fascinating history of the Roman Empire will be explored whilst playing the game!

Picdoku is a brand new pictorial version of the Sudoku craze that has gripped us all! It can be played individually or competitively by 2 players or teams. Each game includes 182 sturdy re-usable tokens and 30 double-sided playing boards so that 60 different games can be played again and again.

Using 45 dominoes, players may play at 1of 3 levels of difficulty while adding fractions to find the sum of 1. Fractional parts are shown in pictorial and numerical form. It's a great activity for home and in learning centers.

Santiago 1898: Final del Imperio is a simulation of the campaign of Santiago de Cuba during the Spanish - American War.

One A3 sized map, 200 cut and paste counters.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description: Introduce children to the great American pastime...BASEBALL while they practice multiplication problems with factors of 3,4,6,7,8, and 9. Multiplication problems determine base hits, singles, triples, home runs, and more. Example: 3X7 = 21 which denotes a single. 4X9 = 36 or a "triple". 2 players or teams may play. Grades 4-8
Battle of Lake Trasimene (217 BC) during the Second Punic war.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description: "1860: I Mille" is a wargame, published on the Italian wargaming magazine "Alea Jacta Est", on the "Expedition of the Thousand", the Garibaldi's campaign against the Kingdom of Two Sicilies.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
The Battle of Adua, 1896: Italy against the Abyssinians. 
"Alea Iacta Est" Italian wargaming magazine, issue 4.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description: Two sets of 30 quadromino tiles provide drill in equivalent fraction recognition at two levels of difficulty. Level 1 includes fractions from 1/4 to 10/12; Level 2 covers 1/4 to 21/24. Each tile shows fractions in numerical form. It's a great activity for individuals, play at home, or in learning centers.

boardgame: So You Think You Know Maine
description:
From the box:
"A game of Maine trivia suitable for natives and people from away."

Players start in Kittery (Southern Maine). Counties in Maine represent different categories. As players roll and move around the board, they must collect cards at each county seat. Players then race to Fort Kent (Northern Maine) to win the game.

Overlord is a "2 player game of pure 3D strategy".

The game is played on a 7x9 board with a crenellated wall located on the middle row between the two players. Both players have 14 pieces located in the back 2 rows on each side of the wall and attempt to capture the opponents pieces by landing on top of them. Pieces move and stack in various ways, and the game is won when one player is reduced to a single stack.

Three levels of difficulty are included in this fast-paced tic-tac-toe styled game. Three game cards are included and players match antonyms from their playing card, to base words found on the spinner. The first person to form a line of antonyms on the board wins the game.

The year is 2340.  Just as in all of history, conquest is at the heart of mankind.  Enslave or become a slave is the future.  You are in charge of breaking through the wall of your enemy’s city to allow the entry of your battalion to take over.  Organic Titazium metal (O37T2y) has been used in the construction of all walls during this time period, which makes them virtually impenetrable.  The wall cubes must be removed carefully or the wall structure will collapse and repair itself thus defeating your mission.  While you are attacking the walls of your enemy, they in turn are attacking you.  By using limited means, you must minimize the damage directed to your city.  Victory belongs to he who enters the city first.

This is a very large book (900 pages) explaining rules to all kinds of games.  The first 300 pages are dedicated solely to card games.  The book then moves on to traditional board and dice games such as checkers, backgammon, and craps.  There are many word and guessing games.  Outdoor activities are also listed, including races, snow or water games, lawn games, and full-blown sports.

Ideas for adults' get-togethers include quick-thinking contests and even dancing games.  The book details countless games for children, suitable for everyday play or social events such as birthday parties.  Some of the party games are geared toward specific holidays.

This huge volume devotes one little paragraph to branded games such as Monopoly, Pit, and Flinch.  It only mentions that they exist, and require special equipment, which is purchased along with the rules.

Although you'll find very familiar entries such as bridge, marbles, and baseball, many of the games are less known, or a variation on a more familiar idea.  Some of the names are intriguing in their own right.  Here's a sampling of names of the many games to learn:

Misery Nap
Bierspiel
Anti-nudist
Hearts of Lettuce
Skeegee-Weegee
The G Man Never Takes His Ease
Literary Salad
Strawberries Without Cream
Schnozzle Race

In Hey, Bartender! everyone is trying to take change from a friendly bartender.  Each player is dealt a hand of seven cards.  The 'bartender' also gets seven cards, played face up on the table.  The object of the game is to trade cards with the bartender, making change such that the bartender gives the player more points than the player gives the bartender.  Play continues until all players have exhausted their hands, at which time the player with the most points wins.

Hey, Bartender! was designed as another version of Pennywise played with cards, and was published in Game Trade Monthly in 1999.

Players learn how to build words using suffixes and base words. The base words are located within the gameboard squares, and the suffixes are listed on the spinner. Students spin and move to the first space where that suffix can be used with a base word to form a word. 245 words can be formed.

Wolf im Schafspelz (Wolf in Sheep's Clothing) is a deduction game, wherein players take turns trying to pick the wolf out from a flock of sheep using sheepdogs.  For those desiring a more complex game, trees are included to break up the sheepdogs' lines of sight.

boardgame: Alternative Games for Those Who Like  a Challenge
description:
From the back cover:

"The alternative games in this booklet are all designed to be played with standard equipment. They offer exciting new dimensions to games played on or with draught boards, cards, dominoes etc., helping to eliminate the constant financial burden of purchasing expensive and often short-lived new games.

The games in Alternative Games for Those Who Like a Challenge are based on popular games that have been modified so they are even more competitive and exciting.
At the same time, they are easy to learn if you already know the standard edition, providing hours of inexpensive entertainment, making this booklet desirable for most game lovers.

Other books designed by Tony Crow are:
Alternative Games for the Family and
Alternative Games for the Studious"

This book contains rules for modified card games, draught board games, Scrabble games and billiard table games.

Black Abyss is a space civilization game where players explore the galaxy for habitable planets and then scan the planets surface for beneficial terrain.  Players will expand their empire by colonizing these planets and erecting buildings that best use the terrain and available space of each planet.  A player will win by means of expansionary, militaristic, diplomatic, or scientific policies.  The game features great details of empire building and fleet management.

"Who was blinded on the road to Damascus?" "Who asked to be tried in Rome?" Players answer these and other questions from the book of Acts as they match these four-sided dominoes. There are two complete sets of dominoes with one set emphasizing people, and the other set emphasizing places. Scriptural references and an answer key is included. 2-4 players. Ages 10 and up

This game reinforces the proper use of grammar as players identify and correct common grammatical errors. Over 700 sentences at three levels of difficulty are included. Throughout the game there are mistakes that are commonly made in the usage of adjectives, adverbs, questions, verbs, subject verb agreement, nouns, comparisons, superlatives, gerunds, infinitives, prepositions and more. A comprehensive answer key is included. Great for all English Language Learners (ESL) low intermediate to advanced and English grades 6-12.

Bible Search is a game of advance and block whose moves are generated by correctly answering the questions, of which there are 3 categories (Beginner, Intermediate and Advanced) in each of 2 sources, the Old Testament and the New Testament.  Correctly answering a question generates 2 choices, a move to advance your pawn 1 space, or a block option which, if successful, allows you to block your opponent(s) progress, or chance missing a turn, advancing 2 spaces or moving back 2 if question incorrectly answered or advancing 1 space or moving back 2 if question incorrectly answered.

With 42 wooden rods ( six color groups of seven rods each) you should try to build as high a tower as possible. The color die decides if you have to put new rods on top of the tower or if you can pass the die to the next player without adding rods.

The game starts with 2 different colored rods placed parallel on the playing surface.

The first player rolls the die, if rolls the same color  as one of the rods on top of the tower, he can pass the die to the next player.

If he rolls a different color as one of the  two rods on top of the tower, he must place to new, differently colored, rods on top of the tower. These have to be placed at a right angle to the others.

You continue to play like this clockwise in turn.

The game is over when the tower collapses. The player who caused the crash loses the game.

This is a good introductory rummy game for children.  The game is for ages 3+ and is for 2-4 players.  There is a game board that has four toy story characters with numbers 1-6.  The idea of the game is to be the player with the most set tokens once the game board is filled.

The game board is filled by either laying a set 3 tiles of same suit in sequence (1, 2, 3 of Woody) or 3 tiles of the same number (4 woody, 4 buzz, 4 what's the horses name again).  You can also add to a set.

There is a wild card in Zurg and there are bonus plays with him.

The Sesame Street version is effectively the same game with a different character-set.

Scale is 240 yards per hex and each unit is a battalion or regiment.  There are also some German companies for the front trenches.  There are two 13x19" maps with over 200 counters. There are two rules books - standard rules that are 5 pages and Messines:1917 rules that run another 4 pages.  There are two scenarios - first day and campaign. Artillery is the centerpiece of the game with four different types of bombardment (saturation,  standard, intermittent, and gas) possible.  There are three types of guns - field, medium, and heavy with each best suited to certain roles.  For Messines the addition of mines makes initial set up and play a real chess match.  The key to victory is correct deployment and effective use of reserves.  For the Allies, one of the key decisions is how much to bombard.  It is very effective but it also destroys the drainage systems of the area and leads to a quagmire that with eliminated possibilities.  For the German player, commitment of reserves is the key.  He has to guess correctly at the main points of attack by the British player before they have sufficiently evolved to make the attack plan apparent.

This trick-taking game is played with a standard deck of playing cards.  Each game, a pair of dice is rolled.  The first die determines how many cards are held in each player's hand (1-6); the other die is special and determines the trump suit.  The second die may come up "no trump" (there is no trump suit) or "no suit" (suit doesn't matter, only face value).  Chips are used to keep score.  Players must ante up before each hand, and the pot is won by the first player to reach eleven points (or to have the highest score after four hands).  Scoring centers around the fact that at start of each game, players use bidding wheels to predict how many hands they can win.  Penalties are applied for guessing incorrectly, while guessing correctly and winning all tricks in a hand can lead to an instant win.

Good stewardship of time, ability, and money is an important part of life. This exciting game introduces children to the principles of good stewardship. As players move around the game board they earn money for a variety of tasks. They learn to put money in savings and in the church offering. There are opportunities to spend money, recycle and do community service projects. A scripture memory component is also included. For 2-4 players. Ages 6 - 12

Your Number's Up is a fast-paced game for 2 to 6 players. The object of the game is to score as many points as possible, by completing simple sums. The first player lays a number card down and subsequent players have to complete the sum. The player completing the sum takes the three cards as their trick and starts the next round. Sounds easy? Well maybe! What happens if you cannot complete the sum? You have to pick up a spare card and wait for your next turn. This game can be played by people with all levels of ability and in our testing, we found some people with devious minds who tried to stop others winning tricks. I'm sure you wouldn't do this! It's fun, but make sure number isn't up by having a handful of cards at the end.

A multi-level maths game to support the national numeracy strategy in an entertaining way. Trilemma tests the times tables and other mathematical functions non-sequentially. This challenging game of chance and skill can be played in a mixed age group with different players choosing different levels within the same game.

This book contains nine new card games mostly played with a regular deck. Some of these games were also published separately.


    Babel - A real time party game that simulates the stock market. This can play a large number of people as long as you have enough card decks to pull it off (1/3 the number of players)



    Leopard - A card game that allows you to sabotage your opponent.



    Auction - An auction game where the playing cards are money.



    Variety - A game that uses bidding to counteract the possibility of getting a bad hand.



    Metamorphosis - A trick taking game where the rules change each hand.



    Switch - A partnership game where the partners switch every hand.



    Eleusis - An induction game.



    Construction - A board game where the board is built with cards.



    Ultima - A fast action chess variant.

Here's a great card game for visual learners! Players match equivalent fractions or find sums of "ONE." Included are cards representing halves, thirds, fourths, sixths, and eighths. Each card pictures a fraction, gives the number word, and shows its corresponding numeral.
Eleven games are included in this domino game. It's a great activity for individual drill on addition and multiplication, or it can be used in a small group in a learning center.
Here's a great way to learn about percentages using dominoes! It's a great game for individuals, or small groups in a learning center.
Subtraction Dominoes provide a unique way to learn subtraction facts with differences from 1 to 18. It's a great learning tool for individual or small group us in a learning center environment.

Battle of the Monsters is a set of several games that can be played using the trading cards included in the Weird n' Wild Creatures collectors set. Weird n' Wild Creatures is a set of colorful data cards covering monsters of nature and mythology. The creatures on the data cards are also depicted on trading cards which list values for attack, defense, and life, which are used to play the Battle of the Monsters games. Some cards also list special powers. The games that can be played with the cards range from the very simple (a war variant where cards and drawn and attack values are compared) to the strategic.

From the tube...
".. a game which truly simulates real harness racing.  You control your horse's speed, decide when to make your move and make all of the other decisions.  Your driving skill - not just luck - determines the outcome."     The object is to drive your horse the farthest twice around the track.

Yes the game is packaged in a tube. The "board" is a piece of fabric backed printed vinyl, similar to a place mat.  Players are assigned or pick a horse from a choice of 200 in the Stable book.  Each horse has a movement number that is broken down into quarters (4 numbers), one for each quarter mile (track is a half mile).  These quarter mile numbers are then reduced to 6 numbers, from 1 to 9.  These 6 numbers are printed on cards.  On his turn the player uses one card to move his horse.  The move is modified by: spaces that count as two, variable length lanes in the curves, swaping cards with the draw pile without changing the movement #, holding back a card till the next quarter mile, blocking another from holding back a card, chance cards at the start and on the home stretch, chance dice roll is required to pass thru a space directly in front of another horse.  It is recommended to have a race secretary.  This person should be a fair minded person who will set up and referee the race.

The players try to use all of their movement while causing others to waste movement.  This player has observed that it is easy for all of the players to use all of their movement with only 6 horses in the race.  All of the movement robbing mechanisms are easily avoided with 6 horses.  The race is then determined by the movement number combined with the Start and Stretch chance cards.  The use of 12 horses makes a much more interesting game.  With 12 horses the movement robbing mechanisms can not be totally avoided.

John Campbell is a Hall of Fame driver (not rider) that holds many of the sport's records.  John Campbell did not design the game but has endorsed it.  The 1989 instructions invite players to a yearly tournament where the championship is decided by playing against John Campbell.

The instructions promote another game, "Classic Thoroughbred Racing" which is not currently in the BGG database.

From the publisher:

Sergeants!™ Scenarios Book 1 contains 23 new scenarios that appear nowhere else. In addition, inside are Map boards 2, 3 and 4 (which have been provided on our website), and are now included professionally printed along with brand-new Map board 6. Requires at least one Sergeants: On the Eastern Front game set.

Included:

4 Map boards

Scenario Booklet
Here's a basic addition activity using a competitive tic-tac-toe format. Three levels of difficulty cover addition problems from 1 to 20. Great for home use or in learning centers.
Here's a basic subtraction activity using a competitive tic-tac-toe format. Three levels of difficulty cover subtraction problems from 1 to 20. Great for home use or in learning centers.
Here's a basic multiplication activity using a competitive tic-tac-toe format. Three levels of difficulty cover basic multiplication problems. Great for home use or in learning centers.
Here's a basic division activity using a competitive tic-tac-toe format. Three levels of difficulty cover basic division problems. Great for home use or in learning centers.

This exciting game sharpens player's addition and subtraction skills as they capture spaces on the board by adding together numerals printed on color-coded disks. Strategy is used to capture other players spaces by exchanging disks on their own space or on an opponent's space. The winner is the player who is the first to capture four squares in one row, one column or one diagonal. Great for two players or teams. Grades 2-8

Extra question cards for the game with the same name.

Expands:

    Genial daneben: Das Spiel

A nautical quiz game where navigators must sail the seas and answer questions about ecology and the rules of the sea.

Themed on the show of the same name on France 3.

Strudel (Eddies) is essentially Parchisi in reverse; players try not to be the first to lose all four pieces to the eddies.  Every turn, players roll two dice, advance any pieces from their start area if possible, and then use any remaining dice to move their own pieces or even whole rings of currents.
The sneaky bats have escaped!  Fly to their rescue with Mother Bat and bring them back to the cave!  With luck and skill, be the first to catch four bats!
Competition and strategy makes this game fun and exciting. Players learn to build words by connecting prefix, suffix, and base words. It's great for two players or two teams. Grades 4-8

3-4 Players expansion to "EinStein würfelt nicht!"

Rules are as in the 2 players version of "EinStein würfelt nicht!", with some modifications:

The game board is 6×6 square fields large.

Each player controls 6 playing pieces i.e. stones with the numbers 1 to 6 marked on them.

The stones of each players are set up in the six fields of one corner of the board.

During his turn a player rolls a die; if he still has the stone with the number corresponding to the rolled value on the board he must move this stone. If the corresponding stone was already removed from play (see below) the player may choose to either move the stone with the next higher or the next lower number available.

In his move a player may move the stone one field horicontally, vertically or diagonally towards the diagonally opposite corner. It is not allowed to move backwards.

If a player ends his move on a field occupied by another stone, be it one of the team partner, opponent or one of his own stones, then this stone is removed from play.

In the 4 players version, the two opposite players build a team each. The team that first occupies the diagonally opposite corner field with a playing piece wins. A team loses if one of the players has no more playing pieces left on the board.

In the 3 players version, the player surrounded by two other players plays a solo against the two other players. If the solist reaches the diagonally opposite corner field, he/she wins. The two opponents win if they beat all solist's playing pieces.

Online Play


     4-player version on DevilPlay  (called "EinStein würfelt nicht - Quattro" there)

The board displays a rainbow with 6 colored tracks. The player pawns are animals. Each animal carries a jewel on its back.
At each turn the player throws a die and moves his/her pawn. A player may move forward or sideways. If the jewel on the back of the animal corresponds with the course of the rainbow, the animal moves faster. Otherwise the animal moves slower.
The first player with all of his/her animals at the other end of the rainbow wins the game.

From the game's website (www.mauerfall89.de):
"Mauerfall 89" is a game with a political background which tries to focus the oblivion of the GDR (former eastern Germany). It deals with the fall of the Berliner wall in 1989. Goal of the game is the fall of the wall (respectively game stones) with demonstrations and attempts to escape - but the regime doesn't sleep...

"Mauerfall 89" was released in 2005 as a limited first edition.

boardgame: Hermann und Thusnelda verprügeln die Römer
description:
From the publisher website:
Hermann und Thusnelda verprügeln die Römer (Hermann and Thusnelda beat up the Romans) deals with the Varus battle in the year 9 AD. In contrast to reality in the game the Romans has a real chance to win...
By request the game rules will also be in Latin!

This game is positioned as a family game it allows players to portray police detectives investigating the robbery or thieves who steal art. The game includes 36 cards that teach the players about different artwork.

The game is inspired by the theft of Munch's Scream, because of this the game has stirred some controversy

"The fast game for long flanks, ideal passing and tactical moves."

Kick-it is a tactical soccer game that challenges the head and not the legs. The team in possession of the ball has 2 movement points available, while the opposing team without the ball has 3 move points available. By jumping straight over the opposing player, the ball can be taken away at any time.
To complete the move, the ball is passed over any number of your own players - preferably, of course, directly into the goal.

"Become a general in the Napoleonic Period and challenge a friend on the battlefield.  Charge! is a non-collectible card game for 2 players.  With Charge! you can revive the emotion of leading an Army and win a battle.  Dispatch your orders to your infantry regiments, fire at the enemy army with your cannons and force it to retreat with your cavalry charges."

A card war game involving advancing your units to break the line of your opponent.  Debuted at Essen 05.

boardgame: Die wilden Fussballkerle: Das dampfhammerharte Fussball-Spiel
description:
"First a wild master, then a world master".

Die Wilden Fussball Kerle now have a full sized soccer board game.  Full teams of eleven players maneuver it out rolling dice and moving their players to get into position to score.

Simple rules and low complexity gear this towards younger players and those not seeking an in depth soccer simulation.

Let's go shopping! Each player begins the game with $200. As purchases are made from the enclosed catalog, players make purchases and compute change using money up to $20. Coin chips from one cent to fifty cents, and currency from $1 to $20 is included. 2-4 players. Grades 4-8

This four-sided domino activity provides drill in addition facts with sums up to 20. It's a great activity for small groups of 2-4 players or for individual use. 120 problem answer combinations are possible with two levels of difficulty. For example, 2+4+3 matches 3+5+1 for a total of 9.

Skim Math - Subtraction is a fast-paced game that uses color-coded problem and answer cards while players race to match addition problems with their corresponding colored answers. For example: 3-1 is blue on the problem card and the blue answer card 2 wins that card. The player with the most cards at the end of the game wins.

Skim Math - Multiplication is a fast-paced game that uses color-coded problem and answer cards while players race to match addition problems with their corresponding colored answers. For example: 4X3 is blue on the problem card and the blue answer card 12 wins that card. The player with the most cards at the end of the game wins.

Skim Math- Division is a fast-paced game that uses color-coded problem and answer cards while players race to match addition problems with their corresponding colored answers. For example: 12 divided by 4 is blue on the problem card and the blue answer card 3 wins that card. The player with the most cards at the end of the game wins.

Skim Math - Addition is a fast-paced game that uses color-coded problem and answer cards while players race to match addition problems with their corresponding colored answers. For example: 3+4 is blue on the problem card and the blue answer card 7 wins that card. that card. The player with the most cards at the end of the game wins.

Players have fun with these unique dominoes by matching coin values in written, numerical, and pictorial form. 45 dominoes and game suggestions are included. This activity may be used for individual or small group play.

Bist Du sicher? (Are you safe?) is a game about life, and the problems that come one's way.  Players draw tiles, resulting in both positive events and successes, as well as setbacks.  However, players can take the cash they collect to buy insurance to negate the negative effects of the setbacks.  It is essentially a multi-player solitaire game, and each player has a private board to track their accomplishments.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description:
Players are on an expedition to discover ancient Mayan temples and capture their treasures. You must help each other on the trek through the jungle, but if you reach a temple site, you must then compete for the rewards. The card game has different phases in which players secretly lay cards to make the expedition's total value exceed the hazards. You can try and trick the others by resting on the journey, but if this means that trek fails, you must pass out your highest cards to the others. Then at the temples, players use their cards to beat each other to the treasures.

The 64 card deck uses authentic b&w illustrations from contemporary explorations of the nineteenth century, which lend the game a strong theme. The cards have simple German text. The cardstock is laminated but flimsy, and while the production is good for a first release, gamers may expect better. However, the price at Essen 2005 was only €4 which was fair. German rules only in the box.

Munera Sine Missione is a free set of miniatures rules which are designed to allow players to recreate gladiatorial combat in the arenas of Ancient Rome. The are designed to give a very quick, simple game, which can be played in ten or so minutes

The game uses a hex grid to regulate movement, and each gladiator has a number of action points to spend each turn. These points can be spent to move or attack other gladiators. Attacks are resolved by a simple roll-off between the attacker and defender, with the attacker getting bonuses for position. The defender gets a save roll to avoid wounds. Play continues until a gladiator loses all of their wounds.

Rules for a wide range of weapons are included,and all major historical gladiator types can be depicted with little problem.
family: Players: Wargames with Rules Supporting Only Two Players

description:
Be the first player/team to score 50 or more points by identifying famous faces randomly assorted in 5 major categories: Movies, Television, & Radio; History; Sports, Recreation, & Leisure; Music, Arts, & Literature; and Miscellaneous.

Includes 400 famous faces cards and instruction sheet

40 creative cards show children's faces featuring numbers that represent the "face value" for each card. A place value spinner focuses attention on the value of the digits on each card. Set A contains numbers that emphasize ones, tens, and hundreds places. For example: If a player's Face Value card number is 945 and the spinner lands on "tens", the player gets 4 point since the 4 is in the tens place. This is a great activity for small group or full class participation. Grades 1-3
Using this unique tic-tac-toe format, students enjoy competitive drill at three levels of difficulty while they learn to recognize fractional parts.
The Aim & Score Arcade Action Game!  With Electronic Light and Sounds.  A game for the entire family!

Box front:

What a wonderful party!  Gather three animal friends and the foods they eat.  Get them to the party first, and win the game!  Fun for the entire family.

Box back:

You're invited to join Zoboo, the loveable lemur, and all his Animal Friends in Animal Junction!  Travel around the colorful gameboard and look for the three animals that are on your special invitation card, plus a food that each of them likes to eat.  After all, it's no party without snacks!

Children will have fun developing matching skills while learning what their favorite animals like to eat.  The first player to get matching cards for all three animals on their card AND one yummy food card for each animal is the winner.

Ages 3 and up.

Financial accountability! With this exciting game, players learn to make purchases and keep financial records of their purchases on their personal financial record sheet. They also have the opportunity to earn money and make strategy decisions while keeping track of their income and cash balance. 2-4 players. Grades 4-8

600 high utility words are contained within the bonus word list and on the gameboard. Words have been carefully selected from current spelling word lists for 4th through 7th grade. Players travel around the gameboard and find words that are misspelled on a list in the square where they land. The game is self-correcting and fun to play. 2-4 Players - Grades 4-7

This self-correcting game reinforces spelling skills from primary to intermediate grades. Players with different learning abilities may play at the same time using an extensive list of spelling words that are included. Weekly spelling lists may be substituted in order to adapt the game to your classroom needs. Grades K-8 Great for two players or two teams.

Conquest Europa is a derivative of Britannia, which deals with the full scope of European history from AD400 (The Fall of the Roman Empire) to AD1480 (The Fall of Constantinople).

As with Britannia players control various historical factions (Romans, Huns, Mongols, Persians, Poles, Ottomans etc) and gain victory points from pre-defined activities (occupying areas, destroying enemy units etc).

The game was manufactured as a pre-production evaluation/test version and was never mass produced by a games company. Approximately 20 copies were hand-manufactured between 1989 and 1992.

25 years on from the first version ... a 2014 Edition is available on BoardGameGeek as PDF download (see below) comprising updated components and some minor rule/scoring changes.
language_dependency: 3: Moderate in-game text - needs crib sheet or paste ups

description: This game comes with two sets of four-sided tiles. There are 30 tiles in each set. Level 1 includes fractions from 1/4 to 5/6: Level 2 includes fractions from 1/4 to 10/12. Fractions are depicted using slices, bars, and numerals. It's a great activity for home use or learning centers.

This four-sided domino activity provides drill in simple column addition facts using 3-digits. It's a great activity for small groups of 2-4 players or for individual use. 120 problem answer combinations are possible with two levels of difficulty.

boardgame: All the Best Drinking Games in the World
description:
Printed by a book editor this is a compendium of about 50 games.. all of them are Drinking Games.
A little comic included for each game  tells us if we are going to play a soft drink one or if we are going to 
get drunk.

Each game has different number of players.

You'll laugh out loud as you and your friends try to fool each other with outrageous stories about your lives. When a card is drawn and a question is asked, the secret roll of the die tells you if your answer should be true or made up.

When you hear a story, bet tokens on whether you think your friend is telling the truth or pulling your leg. You'll win tokens if you're right but lose them if you're wrong. Think you know your friends? How well do they know you? Bluff other players but don't let them fool you. Score enough tokens and you win!

Tell me about...

a time in your life
you would repeat.

something you got away with as a kid.

something you learned about life from
your mother.

Recommended age:  Ages 13 and up

Now the award-winning "You're Pulling My Leg!!" has been adapted for kids and families. You'll laugh out loud as you and your friends try to fool each other with wacky answers to outrageous questions. When a card is drawn and a question is asked, the secret roll of the die tells you if your answer should be true or made up.

When you hear a story, bet tokens on whether you think your friend is telling the truth or pulling your leg. Watch out! You'll win tokens if you're right but lose them if you're wrong. Think you can fool your friends? Don't let them fool you! Score enough tokens and you win!

Tell me about...

a time when you couldn't stop laughing.

something gross you've eaten.

a time when you thought you might get in trouble but didn't.

Recommended age:  Ages 9 and up.
family: Versions & Editions: Junior Versions of Grown-Up Games

description: As players move around the gameboard they identify parts of speech in 125 sentences within the spaces on the board. Nouns, pronouns, verbs, adverbs, prepositions, and articles are addressed.

From the translated rules:

The players are harness race drivers. Each driver tries to maneuver  his team into a good racing position using the colored dice and by the skillful play of 'spurt' cards. The player with the highest total of points after three races over different distances is the "Derby Winner".

An easy to grasp set of miniature rules utilizing;
pre-plotted movement, national characteristics, realistic wind effects, detailed gunnery and even shore batteries.
The base set of rules (35 pages including scenarios, ship design, optional rules and examples) came with 3 sheets if unpunched cardboard ship counters (British, French & Spanish) they later published a set of fleet counters to allow the re-fighting of Trafalgar.

In overall complexity I would say it is slightly higher that Wooden Ships & Iron Men from AH, movement plotting is pretty much the same, what is more detailed is the gunnery and range of possible actions for the ships.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description: This real-life game is fun to play and introduces players to the reality of saving money through store sale. Learn to use percentage calculations while figuring the differences between regular and sale prices of household goods while shopping. Each player selects 24 product cards and during the game may sell them to other players. 2-4 players. Grades 3-8

a 1962 Golf game with 25 individually rated golfers of the time by Nemadji Game Company of Duquette Minnesota. There are several charts (Tee-off, Fairway, Off-Green) that you (representing the golfer) use to move your golf ball (marked by a Tee) down the 9-hole course. The individual player cards are only used once you are on the green. There is a spinner in the bottom of the box where you get a result of 1 to 25 and look up a result on a chart or on the player card. 
Some of the golfers included: 1942 Byron Nelson, 1937 Byron Nelson, 49 Sam Snead, 54 Snead, 53 Ben Hogan, 51 Hogan, 35 Gene Sarazen. I think this was a set of All-stars and might have been an extra option. There is also a set of 1962 golfers: Weiskopf, Crampton, Dent, Wadkins, Watson, Player, Floyd, Nicklaus, Trevino and others.

Swipeout Ski Challenge is three games in one, a card game, a board game and combined card and boardgame.

When you play the full Swipeout Challenge game (The combined card & boardgame), you build up your resources for your mountain adventure through a competitive card game.  You also acquire carious action cards, known as Game Cards, which will help you or hinder other during the boardgame that follows.

The winner is the player with the most money left at the end of the game.

Quick! You've got 90 seconds to write a story about a musician, a phone booth and the desert. Make sure you use the words "juggle," "tasty" and "rebel" and write it in the theme of "time travel." It doesn't have to make sense - the wackier the better! If your story is the favorite, you'll win the challenge.

JabberJot is the hilarious word game where you create all kinds of wild tales. In each round, you race against the timer to jot out a story using three pictures, three words and a theme that are different each time.

When time is up, you'll win the challenge if the jabber (the player judging) likes your story the best. You'll fall on the floor laughing when you hear the outrageous stories your friends and family concoct. Score enough challenge cards and you win the game!

Nemesis is a (collectible) card game about gladiators, set in ancient Rome.
Each deck represents a type of gladiator (Myrmillo, Dimachaera  and Retiarius decks were made) and the total of cards in a deck determine a gladiators total life points. Each turn a player takes cards from his life deck into his hand and attacks other players. Damage is taken by discarding cards from your hand or your life deck. The last player to have cards left in his/her life deck wins the game.
There is a pile of "Arena" cards as well that determine random events that occur in the Arena - some good, some bad.

Game was handmade, monochrome drawings on colored cardboard cards. Some 200 copies were made of the "basic set" (rules, Arena cards and two gladiator decks; Myrmillo and Dimachaera) and the "first expansion set" (Retiarius).
designers: Steve Sic Evans van der Harten

description:
Cien Cañones por Banda is a set of rules that allows to simulate fast but elegantly any naval battle of the 1750-1830 period. The battles range from the Napoleonic wars to the battles of South American independence to the American Revolution. The ships represented, rated for crew, crew morale, firepower, speed and hull, range from the nimble frigates to the Victory, Nelson's flagship at Trafalgar to the biggest sail and wood ship-of-the-line ever built, the 130-gun, 4-decker Santísima Trinidad, also known as "The Escorial of the Seas". the admirals represented include Gravina, Nelson or Villeneuve.

In this first installment of Cien Cañones por Banda there are ships, scenario rules and leaders to simulate the following battles:

Cape St. Vincent 1797 "The Prelude" 
Finisterre 1805: "Brothers in Arms"
Trafalgar 1805: The Abyss of an Empire 
Trafalgar 1805: "The Gravina Touch" (hypothetical scenario)
family: History: Latin American Wars of Independence

description:
Alphabet Runner  combines four games in one - a word, memory, board and card game - and a mix of skill and luck helps children with literacy and numeracy. The game helps children build and memorize three, four and five words and reinforces spelling, turn-taking and concentration.

Alphabet Runner promotes these skills:
Social interaction,
Communication skills,
Speaking and listening,
Small movements,
Hand-eye co-ordination,
Literacy skills,
Mathematical thinking,
Problem solving.

In The Yoga Garden Game you roll a die and move a bumblebee through a circular garden. If you land on a yoga space, you try to do a classic yoga pose or invent your own. This game is for teaching young children Ages 4 and up about yoga.

From Publisher's website at http://www.creativeeduaids.com/whats.htm:

"Senior version of our very popular game in which the players spell the words correctly to move around the board and score maximum marks.  A highly challenging and educative game to learn spelling, pronunciation and improve vocabulary.  Contains and beautiful multi-color play board and 300 spelling cards with 900 spelling of varying difficulty levels."

Components:
1 Board
4 Playing pieces
300 Spelling Cards
1 Special dice

"To buy...or not to buy?" Shopping is a part of every-day life. Finding the best bargain is always a challenge. In this game, players experience the challenges of comparative shopping and determining savings on sale items. Players also make payments, and record financial transactions. Currency from $1 to $55 is included. 2-4 players. Grades 4-9

Going out to eat can be an expensive venture, but with Menu Math, players learn how to keep within their budget and interpret menus. Each player receives $60 and six menus. Choices range from short order restaurants like Grandma's Kitchen, to Chinese, Mexican, and Italian restaurants and dinner houses. There are two levels of difficulty in the game, and it 's great for use at home, or in learning centers. 2-4 players Grades 4-9

Three levels of difficulty are included in this fast-paced tic-tac-toe styled game. Three game cards are included and players match synonyms from their playing card, to base words found on the spinner. The first person to form a line of synonyms on the board wins the game.

This games simulates one year gardening: You start in January and roll your pawn through the seasons. 
You also draw cards with crops and flowers. If you are in the right month you can plant or harvest them

This excellent proofreading game helps players look for mistakes in spelling, word usage, capitalization, and punctuation. With an easy-to-follow scoring system, this self-correcting game is great for any language arts program.

Let's go to the fair! This fun and exciting game helps players learn to manage their money when they go to the fair. After paying the entrance fee, players race around the board to be the first to ride all five rides. The winner must finish first and be able to reconcile any remaining cash. Two levels of difficulty are included. 2-4 players. Grades 2-6

The Birthday Party has not been going well. The soup was lumpy and the main course was stodgy and overcooked. Now dessert is served—the table is laden with Chocolate Mousse, Rice Pudding, Cherry Pie, Fruit Salad and so much more. The guests start to grin wickedly…

The object of Splat! is to furiously flinging food at other players while dodging food they are throwing back at you. The more food that hits you, the messier you get. At the end of the game, the cleanest players are allowed to think that they’ve won.

The game uses three types of cards:

Food Cards : Each food card has a special power which takes effect when the food is either eaten, or is thrown at another player and hits.  Some of these powers are long lasting while others are instant (e.g. the banana split is vitamin fortified and so increases a players hand size, while the revolutionary cupcakes contain added additives which force you to throw all your food at other players). Food cards are either green (generally beneficial powers) and red (generally not beneficial).

And Then Cards : Event cards which can be played during the food throwing process to impact the course of the game.  These cards act as interrupts which provide much of the interactive story telling character of the game (e.g. Amy throws Birthday Cake at Clare but Bob catches it and throws it back at Amy on his next turn). These cards can also be played as dodges to avoid food that is thrown at you.

Object Cards : Throughout the game players may also stand in front of objects (e.g. The Children, The Gnu, Uncle Harry or The Mirror).  If a player standing in front of an object dodges then the food hits the object instead and the thrower gets yelled at by either the Host or the Hostess--forcing them to discard all their And Then cards and eat a food (if they have one).

Each player has a 'You' card with 5 hit locations--when food hits someone the thrower rolls to see where (Head, 2 x Arms, 2 x Legs, 1's miss).  Food which hits a location that is already messy replaces the previous food.

The Butler (Jeeves) also has a hit location card--players may choose to throw food at him through the game and in addition any food that is thrown which does not hit a player or an object may hit Jeeves instead.  Once Jeeves is completely covered with food he chases the players out of the house, ending the game.

Splat! is a reworking of Ed Carter's 1992 food-fight card game, Kersplatt!

Each player gets 18 discs of a color and places them face up  in front of him. Alternating the players place now the discs on the board. The first disc could be placed entirely free, all further must be placed in the row or the column where the last chip has been placed. If one has a higher sum than the fellow player thereby in a row, then you turns all face up chips of your opponent face down and if your (if there are any) face down chips face up. The game ends if no player could place a new chip according to the rules. The player with the highest face up total wins.

The board is made from solid wood and stacked high representing the Tower of Babel. Each player is given a secret color.  On your turn you roll the die and move pawns of any color around one of the rings. When rolling the die you choose to use either the top or bottom number of what was rolled and then you give the other number to the next player for them to move a pawn. As pawns are grouped together they get to move up to higher levels of the tower. When you get pawns to the top you score points based on the position of your color.

Here's a great way to deal with problem spelling. Two students or two teams play this self-correcting game as they properly spell 200 words from cards, using combinations of ei and ie...ir and us...ee and ea, and oa and ou. Grades 4-8

Line-making cardgame (including 2 kind of games).

Number of cards' lines is default according to player-number( or kinds of game).
In your turn, you must place a card to a line from your hand. But point on card must be next to former one (excepting first card of the line).

There are 3 kind of special cards (illustrated like Japanese).

Happy Dog: Wild card. Tie breaker. Its point is same as last card or next card card. After Happy Dog, any card can be placed.

Phoenix: bring back one of your cards from any line to your hand. Or, stop someone from one line placing card.

Dragon: Remove an end card from any line.

At the end of game, a winner is determined by scoring each line. Each player sums points on her/his cards at the line. The player with the most points wins the line and take all cards of the line.
After determining winners of every lines, the player with the most cards (Except phoenixes) wins the game.

Every second counts in this action packed game of strategy and tactics, as each player attempts to take control of the battlefield and become absolute commander. Skill, cunning and tactics are the only thing that will see you through! If you think you have nerves of steel, and the strength of character to make men take the battle to the enemy under a hall of withering fire, then maybe you can be 'Battle Group Commander'! Soften the enemy up first with pop up attacks on any unit that is getting too close with the helicopter, or bombard longer-range targets with the launcher. Try and take out as much of the enemy specialists as soon as possible, but remember while you are doing all that to your enemy, they will be trying to do the same to you!

Arrr, yes!  The legendary treasure of the Pirate Admiral…  They say it’s buried somewhere on Kelly’s Island, it is…  But where the island is, and where t’ dig for it once ye get there, I couldn’t tell ye…  Unless I was… properly persuaded, if ye get m’ meaning…

The object of Ice Pirates is to sail through the frozen waters of Conception Bay in order to find and dig up the Pirate Admiral’s treasure and stash it somewhere no other pirates (your opponents) can find it.

Conception bay is laid out as a board of cards with Bell Island in the middle and named ports around the outside. Each port offers a particular ship upgrade that will assist players in their quest to find and keep the treasure (e.g. Cannons at Harbour Grace, Ice Breakers at Cupids Harbour, Telescopes at Pouch Cove).

The rest of the board starts the game face down--hidden in the bay are three lost islands (where you can find treasure maps) and Kelly's Island where Peter Easton (the Pirate Admiral) had his secret base and (according to legend) left buried treasure when he left for warmer climes. The first player to find, or steal all three treasure maps and reach Kelly's Island gets to dig up the treasure--at which point the game switches to a treasure run with every other player attempting to steal the treasure off them.

Most of the bay cards contain icebergs, which block your movement if they come up in the wrong places, and three shipwrecked mariners who will join your crew if you find them and rescue them--increasing your fighting strength.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
The Arcane Warfare Project encompasses a series of miniature games for the Ancient and Medieval periods.

Arcane Warfare (AWr) versions 1 to 3, from 2004, used conventional opposing dice rolls with d6. The engine was similar to other games, yet it featured a significantly different historical interpretation of the units' behaviour, therefore completely different outcomes.  Out of print.
Renamed
AWR the 4 and 5th editions, also using mainly dice, on a 120 com x 90 cm table. Out of print.

ABC Arcane Battle Composer, is a very fast game using dice and a resolution grid, on a small 90 cm x 60 cm table. No longer supported.

AWE Arcane Warfare Excel, versions 1 and 1.1 are the project's top evolution.
AWE is an original gaming system dialog based, using hidden tiles under the miniature's bases. No dice, cards or markers are necessary to play.
A pure diceless miniature wargame. The play area is a 120 cm x 90 cm table.
First published in 2016, it is currently (2023) the only available title, in it's 1.1 implementation: 
https://boardgamegeek.com/boardgame/209756/arcane-warfare-excel-awe10-field-battle-rules-300b
It can be obtained from Wargame Vault.

Current Work:
On the bench is AWE mini, a game played on a 10 cm square grid using the revolutionary Combat Tiles. All AWE games will require bases preparation with magnetic sheets and steel paper. It is driven by a unike engine, directed for the few wargaming people that seek to play miniatures games not centered in dice rolling /card drawing.
The mini game will require just 90 cm x 90 cm and the big novelty is that it will include built in mechanisms for solo playing. This will require some randomization, but the two players version will still be diceless.

Players learn to put sentences together correctly as they choose simples words that represent "Who", "What", "Where", and "When". Players take turns spinning the spinner. They select a word that is color-coded to the spinner. Once they have collected a "Who", "What", "Where", and "When" card, and can make a sentence, they win! The game includes two levels of difficulty. Level 1 includes words representing "Who", "What", "Where", while level 2 includes words representing "Who", "What", "Where", and "When".

From Spanish publisher El Cinturón de Hierro, "El Salvador 1992" was intended as a wargame from a leftist perspective.  The game covers one year of fighting between the forces of the FMLN(Frente Farabundo Martí de Liberación Nacional) and those of the El Salvadoran government, following a hypothetical breakdown of the 1992 peace talks.

This first expansion for Elixir provide new spells, new magic items, a new transaction and a set of component.
A new card named Alambic help to cast spell.

Expands

    Elixir

description: This four-sided domino activity provides drill in subtraction facts. It's a great activity for small groups of 2-4 players or for individual use. 120 problem answer combinations are possible with two levels of difficulty.

This four-sided domino activity provides drill in multiplication facts covering numbers from 0 to 9 . It's a great activity for small groups of 2-4 players or for individual use. 120 problem answer combinations are possible with two levels of difficulty. For example, 3X4 matches 2X6 or 12.

This four-sided domino activity provides drill in division facts with divisors of 1 to 9. It's a great activity for small groups of 2-4 players or for individual use. 120 problem answer combinations are possible with two levels of difficulty.

Here's a great game that incorporates a baseball theme using first through sixth grade level spelling words. At three levels of difficulty, students with different learning abilities may play in pairs or small groups in a learning center. Get the whole class involved by creating an overhead transparency from the black-line master included. The game may also be adapted for weekly spelling and vocabulary drill, as well as remedial classes. Grades 1-6

boardgame: Shadows over Camelot: Sir Bedivere, the 8th Knight
description:
The 8th Knight was part of the (German) Spielbox-Magazin Number 3/2005.

It was also included in:
- Game Master Magazine, issue n°0 (Italian)
- Jeux sur un Plateau, issue n°16 (French)
- Games International issue n°22.

Subsequently, the miniature along with the character card and an orange die appeared in Shadows over Camelot: Merlin's Company.
language_dependency: 2: Some necessary text - easily memorized or small crib sheet

description:
In 1885 American humorist and author Samuel L. Clemens (Mark Twain) received a patent for Mark Twain's Memory Builder, a game designed to help players retain important dates and facts from throughout history.
Points are awarded by what type of fact is given: 10 for an Accession of kings, presidents, and other rulers; 5 for Battles; and 1 for minor events such as births, deaths, and inventions. Additionally, one point is awarded for facts not related to dates, such as the number of bones in a human foot.
The board consists of a table with areas marked 1-100 representing the years of any century with 3 rows of dots for the three kinds of facts previously mentioned and a separate section for un-dateable facts. As a player names a fact he places a pin in the related pinhole. Anything worth remembering is admissible.
Play continues in turns for a previously agreed upon amount of time.

From the back of the game box:

Winnie the Pooh and his adorable friends are having a fun filled busy day. Join them in their adventures around the Hundred-Acre Wood. Play as Winnie the Pooh, Piglet, Eeyore or Tigger in this "Busy Day Race Game". The aim of the game is to be the first player to arrive HOME. As you race around the board you will come across some obstacles which will help and hinder you on your journey home.

This second expansion for Elixir provide some new spells and magic items.

The big improvement is "places" (cards giving to his owner a free component) and panels (cards indicate if a place is open or not)

This expansion no longer exist alone.  You can find it in the box called 'la Compil' with the two others expansion.

description:
This is an Italian game by Gastone Fiori.

Look like 3D Checkers with 3 transparent octagonal boards.
Put one over the other like a tower.

There are 3 kinds of pieces; a diagonal one, a concave one and a convex one (that perfectly fit the concave shape.

The convex one appears like a little House

You put 2 concave, 1  convex and four diagonal pieces on the line Cross..

All the piece can eat like Checkers in the same board.. but can eat in different mode on parallel board according with their shape. Convex and concave pieces may "Kill" pieces on other boards if they are in a "reflected diagonal" with an opponent piece.. diagonal pieces may kill pieces on other boards if they form a diagonal with the opponents piece.

In Build! You have to play the role of a pitiless businessman who wants to build up as many construction projects as he can, regardless of their nature and without caring about the damages they can deliver to the environment and to public health. To achieve your goal, you can use different kinds of Cards: Land Cards, on which you can build up constructions; Construction Cards, representing the different buildings you can put on your land; Actions Cards (Attack & Defense), which have the objective of damaging the enemy and/or defending yourself from enemy attacks; Catastrophes, which are terrible events that could happen and damage all the players in the game (Eg. Earthquakes, Volcanic Eruptions, etc.)
All the card illustrations are very funny, the rules are easy and the playtime is about 15-20 minutes, but you can add some strategy to the game, using all the rules included in the game, so that playing time is increased to 30 minutes. 
The game box includes two card decks, (each of 55 cards) and the rules. 
The size of the card is 8,7 x 6,3 cm.
The game is designed for 2-8 people.

This trivia game is based on 20th Century Fox movie, The Rocky Horror Picture Show. Recommended for Ages 17 and up.

From the box:

It's just a jump to the left...to play the Rocky Horror Trivia Game. Pick one of over 1,200 questions about your favorite transsexual movie and its stars. "It's astounding!"

And then a step to the right...to roll the custom die and earn 5,000 Rocky points to win the game, which "is simply a figment of your imagination."

Put your hands on your hips...and do the pelvic thrust. Drive your opponents insane as you relive this sinful experience and immerse yourself into the twisted world of Rocky Horror to earn bonus points.

The true fans, not virgins, of this horrific, sci-fi, B-movie, are invited to celebrate the 30th Anniversary of one of the most exciting and entertaining films of all time. Test your knowledge..."Hot Patootie, bless my - _____." Act out your favorite character in Wild And Untamed Thing. Dance The Time Warp, and enjoy erotic trivia game fun..."guaranteed to thrill you, chill you and fulfill you!"

Contains: 250 trivia cards, 1,231 Questions, score pad, oversized die, and rules.

The game is set in a mixed science/fantasy setting, with both metal robots and dragons. Depth of game play is claimed to be one of its features.

Starmada X: Brigade is a source book for The Universal Game of Starship Combat, providing background and starship designs for seven different factions, each taken from Brigade Models' excellent line of starship miniatures.

The 72 different ship classes included in this book have each been designed by players of Starmada. They present a wide variety of philosophies and strategies, illustrating the flexibility of the most comprehensive starship construction system available in any space combat wargame.

Brigade includes the complete rules for Starmada X X, allowing you to start blasting your opponents into just so much space dust right away!

description:
Become the richest collector!

A points scoring game of ‘facing capture’, ‘waiting capture’ ‘void captures’ & ‘capture proper’ for 2 to 8 player’s played on an classically designed, yet attractively different, checkered board where war is fought IN every direction & FROM every direction!

A Classic in Waiting, in Tradition, and in Style, Babylonia™ is a social 2 to 8 players points scoring capture game, where the winner is the player with the most point at the end of the game after deductions have been made. In this game the player’s men are called ‘Zealots’.

Taking turns, a move is to lay your Zealot on the board, then totaling your scores, if any, for that turn - and that’s about it really, but don't be misled; from humble beginnings the whole board will soon have you on your toes! So watch out. You have been warned!!!

Pizza Box Football's 32-Team Expansion Game is based on the 2004-2005 season.  All 32 teams are modeled after their strengths and weaknesses.  How will you shut down Indy's powerful passing? Penetrate Pittsburgh's stalwart defense? Slow Atlanta's running QB?  Can anybody beat New England?

The expansion comes in its own envelope, allowing it to store easily inside your existing Pizza Box Football game.  The envelope holds the cards for the 32 teams as well as the expansion play sequence cards.

Autoduel America puts the world of 2039 right at your fingertips. Two beautiful 23" x 35" full-color maps detail the North American continent, including road conditions for the major interstate, U.S., and state highways; nuclear zones; truck stops; cities; fortress towns; ruins; military bases; national forests and parks; state and national capitals; no dueling zones and lawless areas. Use them as posters or as game aids for Car Wars or GURPS Autoduel campaigns."
